article_title: Western States Race to Adopt Wildfire Building Codes as Fire Season Approaches, but Implementation Gaps Remain

content_preview.title: Western States Race to Adopt Wildfire Building Codes as Fire Season Approaches, but Implementation Gaps Remain
content_preview.summary: Colorado, California, and Oregon are all enforcing or adopting wildfire-resistant construction standards in 2026, though municipal capacity and cost concerns threaten to slow the rollout.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

As the 2026 wildfire season approaches, three western states are converging on a shared strategy: mandating that new homes be built to resist fire. Colorado's Wildfire Resiliency Code reached its April 1 adoption deadline for local jurisdictions in wildland-urban interface areas. California's consolidated WUI building code took effect on January 1, introducing the state's strictest fire-hardening requirements to date.
